steps.title: Identify the contextual signal
steps.content: The text describes a research shortcoming (myopic focus on a few wealthy regions) due to limited data. The blank describes what researchers later did with that shortcoming "after gaining new access" to data from Africa and Eastern Europe — i.e., they fixed or improved it.

steps.title: Match the precise word
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean fix or improve — researchers used new data to address the myopic shortcoming. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — postulate: "Researchers would later postulate this shortcoming" — postulate means assume or propose as a hypothesis. That would mean claiming the shortcoming exists, not fixing it. ✗
  • Choice B — ameliorate: "Researchers would later ameliorate this shortcoming" — ameliorate means make better or improve. New access to African and Eastern European records let them fix the myopic regional focus. ✓
  • Choice C — sanction: "Researchers would later sanction this shortcoming" — sanction means officially approve or penalize. Neither meaning fits correcting a research flaw. ✗
  • Choice D — rationalize: "Researchers would later rationalize this shortcoming" — rationalize means justify or explain away. This implies defending the flaw, not correcting it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
